title: cordyline terminalis var. cannifolia
intro: Cordyline terminalis var. cannifolia, also known as the cabbage palm or cabbage tree, is a variety of the cordyline plant that is native to Australia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which have been attributed to the presence of saponins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cordyline terminalis var. cannifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
